content.heroQuote: Does that Currys banner actually move the needle — or are we paying for exposure we can't measure?
content.heroSubtext: Loretta, Samsung's sell-in, sell-out, brand and competitive data all live in separate systems. Milo pulls them together so your team can query across all of it. No retailer calls, no BI backlog.

keyMetrics.label: to run a full cross-dataset correlation
siloed data sources unified in one query

clientQuestions.question: Which of our Currys and Argos banner campaigns drove measurable sell-out uplift — and which are we paying for with no return?
Where is the gap between what we're shipping from factories and what's clearing off retail shelves — and which SKUs are accumulating risk?
Which products across our TV and appliance range are cannibalising each other — and where is our complexity costing us margin?
Do the weeks we increase media spend show movement in consideration and purchase intent — and does the pattern vary by category?
Which Galaxy phone buyers in our registered customer database are most likely to convert to a Samsung TV or appliance next — and what signals predict it?
Which ClickSense data sources are clean enough to connect today, and what does Milo need to get the rest analysis-ready?

solutionHighlights.benefit: Milo runs inside Samsung's own infrastructure. Nothing leaves, and you're not locked into our platform.
Query factory-to-retailer and retailer-to-consumer data together. See the gaps before they hit the quarterly review.
Correlations, predictions, pattern recognition across your brand, marketing and competitive data. The analysis your team keeps putting off.

relevantCaseStudy.result: Milo spotted low Coco Pops stock across Dubai stores and identified which SKUs drove the highest sell-through — things that previously took weeks to surface.
